what shows branching papillae having flbrovascular stalk covered by a single layer of cuboidal cells having ground-glass nuclei?
per_sample.predicted_answer: This is a histological image of the bronchial tree. The branching papillae are the terminal branches of the bronchial tree, and the flbrovascular stalk is the stalk that connects the papilla to the bronchial wall. The single layer of cuboidal cells covering the stalk is characteristic of the bronchial epithelium. The ground-glass nuclei are a feature of the bronchial epithelium, which is composed of cuboidal cells with a ground-glass appearance due to the presence of a dense network of collagen fibers. Therefore, the image shows the bronchial epithelium.

what is involvedd in pathogenesis of two main types of diabetes mellitus?
per_sample.predicted_answer: The pathogenesis of type 1 and type 2 diabetes mellitus involves a combination of genetic and environmental factors. In type 1 diabetes, the immune system attacks and destroys insulin-producing beta cells in the pancreas, leading to a lack of insulin. In type 2 diabetes, the body becomes resistant to insulin, and the pancreas cannot produce enough insulin to maintain normal blood glucose levels.
